name: ANTHEM completed but has no posted results in the current registry record
summary: ANTHEM is a near-term human aging-biomarker watch item, not evidence of benefit until results are posted or published.
statement: The ANTHEM registry lists a completed phase 3 trial of 12-week metformin or placebo in 101 adults aged 40 to 75 years, stratified by insulin-sensitivity status, with insulin-sensitivity, mitochondrial-function, glucose-regulation, and aging-biomarker endpoints, but no posted results as of its April 23, 2026 update.
population_or_model: Adults aged 40 to 75 years, free of chronic disease, stratified by insulin-sensitive or insulin-resistant baseline status
time_horizon: Registry first posted February 11, 2020; actual completion March 12, 2025; last update posted April 23, 2026
caveats: Registry status can lag publication or sponsor reporting.
The registry cannot support benefit, harm, or stage-upgrade claims without posted or published results.
The trial is short-term and biomarker-focused rather than a frailty, multimorbidity, or survival outcomes trial.
